comment: Synthetic 3L axi-cel candidate. Distinct from existing patient_dlbcl_chemorefractory_for_cart.json (which is pre-decision CAR-T candidate from 1L R-CHOP → 2L R-ICE / chemorefractory archetype). This case is the post-decision continuation of the H2 line-of-therapy chain: 1L R-CHOP CR → late relapse → 2L Pola-BR → progression at 7 mo → 3L axi-cel administration plan. Age 56 fit ECOG 1, CD19+ confirmed, no CNS disease. Engine routes ALGO-DLBCL-2L: standard-track DEFAULT = IND-DLBCL-2L-POLA-R-BENDAMUSTINE (step 3 false branch fallback), aggressive-track ALTERNATIVE = IND-DLBCL-3L-AXICEL-CART (ZUMA-1; ORR ~83%, CR ~58%, 5y OS ~43%). The 3L axi-cel intent surfaces correctly as the aggressive-track curative-intent option presented to the MDT. KB-DRIFT NOTE: this case sets line_of_therapy=2 because ALGO-DLBCL-2L applicable_to_line_of_therapy=2, even though clinically this is 3L (post 2 prior lines); existing chemorefractory_for_cart.json sets line_of_therapy=3 and consequently fails algorithm match at baseline (exit=1, Algorithm: None) — there is no ALGO-DLBCL-3L in KB. Surfacing this drift: KB should add ALGO-DLBCL-3L or generalize line_of_therapy matching to ≥. Secondary drift: ALGO-DLBCL-2L step 1 references RF-ASCT-ELIGIBLE-COMPOSITE which doesn't fully model post-decline state, so step 2 axi-cel branch never fires deterministically as default (gated on multiple findings + RF wiring gaps noted in algo notes).

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma, NOS — relapsed/refractory after 2 prior lines

transplant_assessment.rationale: Already declined autoSCT pathway at 2L (Pola-BR chosen instead); current relapse is post-2 lines → CAR-T pathway

cart_eligibility.cart_center_referral: international (Germany / Poland / Israel; UA referral pathway)
cart_eligibility.bridging_plan: rituximab + low-dose corticosteroid only (avoid lymphodepletion until LD chemo)
